subject: UX & Research
title: The Zune Launch Freeze
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Zune Launch Freeze.

hook.text: On December 31, 2008, every single Zune 30 player froze simultaneously. Microsoft's iPod rival was bricked.

buildup.text: A clock driver bug couldn't handle the last day of a leap year. The devices entered an infinite loop.

discovery.text: The fix? Wait until midnight on January 1st. The bug resolved itself when the calendar rolled over.

twist.text: Microsoft's support site crashed under the traffic. Users couldn't even find the 'just wait' instructions.

climax.text: Edge cases in date handling are notorious UX killers. Test leap years, time zones, and boundary conditions.

punchline.text: Always test edge cases. Dates and boundaries will surprise you.

quiz.question: What caused every Zune 30 to freeze simultaneously?
options: A clock driver bug that couldn't handle the last day of a leap year
A manufacturing defect in the hardware
Users all downloaded the same corrupted update
